jtbds.title: Conduct KYC customer due diligence
jtbds.situation: new customer onboarding triggers KYC/CDD requirement; analyst must verify identity through primary sources, screen against sanctions and PEP lists, assess risk rating, and document findings before account activation
jtbds.motivation: meet BSA/AML CDD rule obligations and prevent financial crime risk
jtbds.outcome: KYC file completed; risk rating assigned; account activated or declined with documented rationale
success_criteria: identity verified through government-issued ID
OFAC, PEP, and adverse media screening completed
risk rating assigned per CDD policy
EDD initiated for high-risk customers
beneficial ownership collected for legal entities

jtbds.title: Investigate AML transaction monitoring alert
jtbds.situation: transaction monitoring system generates alert on unusual activity; investigator must review transaction history, assess structuring or layering indicators, obtain customer explanation, and determine whether to clear or escalate to SAR
jtbds.motivation: detect and report suspicious activity while minimizing false positives that burden legitimate customers
jtbds.outcome: alert cleared with documented rationale or escalated to SAR filing team
success_criteria: alert reviewed within 5 business days
all relevant transactions reviewed (lookback period applied)
customer contact attempted if appropriate
SAR decision documented with supervisor concurrence

jtbds.title: File Suspicious Activity Report (SAR)
jtbds.situation: AML investigation concludes that activity meets SAR filing threshold; compliance officer must complete FinCEN SAR form, obtain BSA officer approval, file via FinCEN BSA e-filing, and maintain confidentiality of filing from subject
jtbds.motivation: meet statutory SAR filing obligation within 30-day deadline while maintaining confidentiality
jtbds.outcome: SAR filed with FinCEN via BSA e-filing; confirmation number retained; filing log updated
success_criteria: SAR filed within 30 days of detection (60 days with extension)
BSA officer approval obtained before filing
filing confirmation number retained
subject not tipped off to filing
continuing SAR filed if suspicious activity continues

jtbds.title: Conduct internal audit engagement
jtbds.situation: internal audit plan calls for review of a business process or control area; auditor must develop engagement scope, conduct fieldwork, document findings, issue draft report, obtain management response, and issue final report
jtbds.motivation: provide independent assurance on internal control effectiveness and risk management
jtbds.outcome: final audit report issued; management responses incorporated; findings tracked in audit management system
success_criteria: engagement letter issued before fieldwork begins
draft report issued within 15 days of fieldwork completion
management response received within 10 days
final report issued within 5 days of response
findings rated by risk level

jtbds.title: Manage compliance policy review and attestation
jtbds.situation: annual policy review cycle requires compliance officer to review and update all compliance policies, route for legal and executive approval, publish to policy management system, and collect employee attestations
jtbds.motivation: ensure compliance policies remain current with regulatory changes and that all employees have read and attested
jtbds.outcome: all policies updated, approved, published, and attested by required employees before deadline
success_criteria: all policies reviewed before annual deadline
legal counsel sign-off obtained for material changes
employee attestation rate ≥ 95% within 30 days of publication
non-attestors escalated to manager

jtbds.title: Manage OFAC/sanctions screening and match review
jtbds.situation: sanctions screening system generates potential match on customer, counterparty, or transaction; compliance officer must review match, apply OFAC guidance, determine true hit vs. false positive, block or release transaction, and report true hits
jtbds.motivation: comply with OFAC SDN list and sanctions programs while minimizing false positives that delay legitimate transactions
jtbds.outcome: match reviewed and resolved; true hits reported to OFAC and transaction blocked; false positives cleared
success_criteria: all potential matches reviewed within 24 hours
true hits reported to OFAC and blocked
false positive rationale documented
blocked funds held in segregated account
OFAC license obtained if applicable

jtbds.title: Manage mandatory compliance training campaign
jtbds.situation: annual or event-triggered compliance training (BSA/AML, code of conduct, data privacy, anti-corruption) must be assigned to all required employees, tracked to completion, and documented for regulatory examination
jtbds.motivation: meet regulatory training requirements and build compliance culture; demonstrate program to examiners
jtbds.outcome: training assigned, completed by ≥95% of employees within deadline, completion records retained
success_criteria: training assigned to all required employees
completion rate ≥ 95% within 30-day window
non-completers escalated to HR for disciplinary action
completion records retained for 3+ years
training content reflects current regulatory requirements

jtbds.title: Manage data subject access or deletion request (GDPR/CCPA)
jtbds.situation: data subject submits access, rectification, erasure, or portability request under GDPR Article 15-20 or CCPA; DPO must verify identity, locate personal data across systems, fulfill or deny request with documented legal basis, and respond within statutory deadline
jtbds.motivation: fulfill data subject rights obligations under GDPR (30-day) and CCPA (45-day) deadlines
jtbds.outcome: request fulfilled or denied with documented legal basis; response delivered within statutory deadline
success_criteria: identity verification completed before data disclosure
data located across all relevant systems
response delivered within GDPR 30-day / CCPA 45-day deadline
legal basis for denial documented if applicable
record of processing maintained

jtbds.title: Manage regulatory examination response
jtbds.situation: regulator (OCC, Fed, CFPB, state) initiates examination and issues document request list; CCO must triage requests, coordinate document production, brief executives, manage examiner meetings, and respond to findings
jtbds.motivation: demonstrate strong compliance program and minimize examination findings; complete response on-time
jtbds.outcome: all examiner requests responded to within deadlines; findings reviewed and MRA response plan submitted
success_criteria: initial document production within examiner deadline
examiner meeting briefings prepared
draft findings reviewed by legal counsel
MRA/MRIA response plan approved by board

jtbds.title: Conduct enterprise compliance risk assessment
jtbds.situation: annual compliance risk assessment requires identifying regulatory requirements applicable to the business, assessing inherent risk and control effectiveness, rating residual risk, and presenting results to board and regulators as evidence of risk-based compliance program
jtbds.motivation: demonstrate risk-based compliance program to regulators and allocate compliance resources to highest-risk areas
jtbds.outcome: risk assessment completed; residual risk heat map presented to board; compliance priorities updated for coming year
success_criteria: all applicable regulatory requirements inventoried
inherent risk and control gap rated for each requirement
residual risk heat map approved by board
high-risk areas have documented remediation plans
assessment updated annually or when material changes occur
